message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Recently SIQR model was proposed by Odagaki as the modification of conventional SIR model by adding the term for isolation of infected persons, Q(Quarantined). The exponent
                  <jats:italic>λ</jats:italic>
                  of the exponential function expressing the number of newly tested positive persons was defined as an linear equation explicitly with three important parameters, transmission coefficient, social distancing ratio x and isolation rate q. In this study, applying this model to the number of positive persons in publicly available database, daily
                  <jats:italic>λ</jats:italic>
                  values are regression analyzed, and social distancing ratio and isolation rate are derived. Analyses for 7 countries including Japan, Taiwan, South Korea, and western countries are performed and determine the dynamic locus of q-x relation on the q-x plane during epidemic propagation. Finally, the remaining parameter, the transmission coefficient is shown to closely relate to the maximum λ, λ
                  <jats:sub>max</jats:sub>
                  , and λ
                  <jats:sub>max</jats:sub>
                  (transmission coefficient) is characterized as a specific value for each country. Then, the magnitude of λ
                  <jats:sub>max</jats:sub>
                  is combined with the value of λ
                  <jats:sub>min</jats:sub>
                  to influence the total number of new cases until the convergence stage.
                </jats:p>
title: Dynamic Analysis of Social Distancing Ratio, Isolation Rate and Transmission Coefficient in COVID-19 Epidemic for Many Countries by SIQR Model

reference.unstructured: T. Odagaki , Bussei Kenkyu@Web Vol.8, No.2.082101(2020), in Japanese, T. Odagaki, https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.06.02.20117341
Our World in Data COVID-19 dataset, https://ourworldindata.org/coronavirus-source-data, (Accessed: 2020.7.12)
